_comment: Single source of truth for CI-plane secret rotation (scripts/rotate_secret.py). Each entry is one rotatable secret and lists every place its value lives: GitHub Actions ENVIRONMENT secrets (github_env_secrets: repo + environment + secret_name), GitHub Actions REPOSITORY secrets (github_repo_secrets: repo + secret_name — the store CI reads when a job declares no 'environment:', e.g. the reusable ci-python-*.yml SDK install; a value written only to environments is invisible to those CI jobs), and/or non-Terraform Vercel project env vars (project_id + env_key). SCOPE: CI-plane credentials ONLY (build/deploy plumbing). App secrets in Terraform's SECRETS_INVENTORY (DB URLs, RSA keypair, KDF_SERVICE_KEY, STRIPE_*, OIDC client secrets, CRON_SECRET, etc.) are Terraform-owned — do NOT add them here; a direct write would drift. If one is ever added, set "terraform_managed": true and the engine refuses to write it and points to the terraform SECRETS_ROTATION runbook. 'kind' is the intended primary mode (paste|generate); 'generator' picks the generate strategy (vercel_token|random_urlsafe); 'per_env' makes generate mint a distinct value per environment; 'shared': true on a vercel_token entry mints ONE token (entry-level 'vercel_token_name') and writes the same value to every target (app/env filters ignored). Vercel entries whose project_id starts with 'TODO' are skipped with a warning.

secrets.name: GH_PACKAGES_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT (contents:read on the private SDK repo) used at build time to pip-install kdf-auth-sdk. GitHub cannot mint PATs via API, so this is paste-only: owner creates the PAT, stages it in the SECRET_VALUE_NEW repo secret, then runs paste. It is distributed to TWO distinct GitHub secret stores that must BOTH be refreshed on every rotation: the per-environment secrets deploys read (github_env_secrets) AND the repository-level Actions secrets CI reads (github_repo_secrets). The reusable ci-python-*.yml jobs run on pull_request with NO 'environment:', so they can only see repository/org secrets — a token pasted solely into the environments leaves CI cloning the SDK with the OLD, now-revoked token ('Authentication failed') while deploys work fine. All three backends (kriegerdataforge, fitness-app-backend, tiffanys-space-backend) install the SDK in CI via the repo-level secret.

secrets.name: VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN
secrets.description: ONE shared, account-scoped Vercel token used EVERYWHERE: every app repo's CI deploys with it AND the terraform repo uses it to MANAGE Vercel (create projects, set env vars) via the Terraform provider. Consolidated 2026-06-30 from the former split (VERCEL_TOKEN deploy + VERCEL_API_TOKEN terraform) — both were the same Full-Account-privilege token, so two separate tokens was pure redundancy. Auto-minted via the Vercel API as a single token, written to every target's GitHub environment secret; the previous token is deleted only if ALL writes succeed (a partial failure keeps both valid so no repo holds a revoked token). App/env filters are ignored — it is one value everywhere. In the terraform repo this secret feeds TF_VAR_vercel_api_token (the provider's variable name is unchanged). Its check.expiry is AUTO-MAINTAINED: after each mint the rotation workflow runs `--mode record-expiry` to stamp today+45d and opens a PR; the weekly monitor then doubles as a watchdog if the cron ever stops.

secrets.name: CICD_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT carrying the full provisioning/fallback scope: Repository Administration + Contents + Environments + Secrets + Variables + Actions + Issues + Pull requests (all read/write) over ALL repos (incl. future), plus Metadata read. The rotation + kit workflows mint ephemeral GitHub App tokens (docs/design/github-app-migration.md) and use CICD_PAT only as the `|| secrets.CICD_PAT` fallback; its sole ACTIVE consumer is issue-create-repo.yml (creating a personal-account repo needs a user PAT — an App token can't, until the org move). GitHub has no PAT-creation API, so it is rotated BY HAND — monitored only (no engine targets). Retirement is tracked in the central roadmap (KDF docs/engineering/GITHUB_FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md, Enhancement 1). Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.3. Last rotated 2026-06-30 with a 30-day token.

secrets.name: VERCEL_MASTER_TOKEN
secrets.description: Full-Account Vercel token the engine uses to mint/delete the shared deploy/management token (VERCEL_DEPLOYMENT_TOKEN). Stays MANUAL (paste-only): minting requires Full Account scope, and this is the bootstrap credential the rotation itself depends on — it can't rotate itself with itself. Rotate it by hand before expiry (recipe §8.4) and update check.expiry below. Monitored only (no engine targets). Last rotated 2026-06-30 with a 30-day Vercel token.

secrets.name: KDF_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
secrets.description: Private key (.pem) for the KDF GitHub App, stored as a kriegerdataforge-cicd repo secret (alongside the non-secret KDF_APP_ID). The rotation + kit workflows mint short-lived installation tokens from it (actions/create-github-app-token) instead of using long-lived PATs. It has no expiry, but rotate it periodically; GitHub Apps support multiple private keys for zero-downtime rotation. Monitored only (no engine targets). Recipe: docs/guides/SECRET_ROTATION.md §8.3a.
